name: The Golf House of Maple Park
about:
The Golf House of Maple Park is an indoor golf facility located in Maple Park, Illinois, offering a focused environment for practice, play, and improvement. The venue features golf simulators available for rental by the hour, providing a space for golfers to stay sharp during the off-season or refine their skills year-round. The facility is managed by Brian King Performance Golf, integrating professional coaching services directly into the simulator experience.

The pricing model is designed to be accessible, with a flat rate of $25 per hour for simulator rentals. Memberships are available for frequent visitors, offering a monthly pass that reduces the hourly rate and provides exclusive 24/7 after-hours access for those who wish to practice on their own schedule. The facility also hosts organized leagues, allowing players to compete in weekly events with online leaderboards.

Beyond casual play, the venue serves as a comprehensive training center. PGA Professional Brian King offers lessons for players of all levels, from beginners looking to break 100 to advanced players aiming to shoot lower scores. The facility also features a dedicated space for ladies' golf lessons and supports various instructional programs aimed at long-term game improvement.
why_golfers_like_it: Professional coaching by PGA Professional Brian King
Organized indoor leagues with online leaderboards
No initiation fees or contracts for memberships

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: The website does not explicitly specify club rentals, so it is recommended to contact the venue or bring your own equipment.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: The facility focuses on training and simulators. While not explicitly prohibited, it is best to inquire about their specific policy regarding outside food and drink.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: Typically, 18 holes on a golf simulator takes approximately 45 to 60 minutes, depending on the pace of play and the simulator software used.

faq.question: Is this kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the facility appears to be family-friendly and offers a 'Ladies Golf Lessons' program, suggesting they welcome various age groups and skill levels.
